payload.summary: Comprehensive framework for finding and evaluating existing community implementations and best practices before building new solutions. Covers systematic search strategies across GitHub, npm packages, Stack Overflow, documentation patterns, and commercial alternatives with evaluation criteria for code quality, maintenance status, and community adoption.
strategy: Identify core requirements and technical constraints of the feature or problem
Search multiple sources: GitHub for implementations, npm/PyPI for packages, Stack Overflow for discussions, official docs for patterns
Evaluate candidates using criteria: code quality, maintenance status (last commit/issue), community size (stars/forks), license compatibility, documentation quality
Select the best solution or combination: use existing if suitable, adapt if close, or build new only after thorough research
Document findings and decisions with clear rationale for traceability
validation: node -e "console.log('Gene validation passed: community solution search framework')"
preconditions: User requests a new feature or solution
No existing implementation known within the project
